# API page: collapsible details for each MCP tool, auto-generated from bridge

## Why

The /api page currently shows only a flat 3-row table listing search/query/execute with no way to see what functions each tool exposes. Users (especially humans pasting prompts into an MCP client) can't tell what's available without reading the source.

We just added news parity (s-f499), which means the prompt string in server.ts now lists ~50 functions. Hand-maintaining a parallel docs string is brittle.

## What

1. Co-locate HOST_FN_DOCS metadata table inside app/mcp/bridge.ts mapping each host function name to { signature, description }. This sits next to the implementations so updates happen together.
2. Export a getHostFunctionDocs() helper that returns { read, execute, undocumented, missing }, computed at request time by intersecting HOST_FN_DOCS with Object.keys(buildReadFunctions()) and Object.keys(buildExecuteFunctions()).
3. New <MCPTools /> client component rendered from api-docs.mdx, replacing the static '### Tools' table. Uses <details>/<summary> matching the existing pattern (border border-harbour-200, no rounded corners).
4. Each tool (search/query/execute) is a top-level <details>. search/query already have rich descriptions baked into server.ts — show those. execute expands to show a sortable table of all imported functions grouped by category (read functions appear under query too since execute is a superset).
5. Loader provides the docs payload. Component is auto-driven — if a new function is added to bridge.ts and forgot in HOST_FN_DOCS it shows up as '(undocumented)' rather than disappearing. If a doc entry has no matching bridge function it shows '(removed)'.

## Acceptance

- /api page renders 3 collapsible cards: search, query, execute.
- Expanding query/execute shows a table of available siliconharbour module imports with signature + short description.
- All ~50 current functions are documented.
- Adding a new host fn to bridge.ts AND HOST_FN_DOCS makes it appear without further changes.
- Missing docs surface as visible '(undocumented)' warnings on the page.
- pnpm run build, pnpm run lint:fix, pnpm test --run all pass.
